Description: Quantization, pruning, and compression techniques to reduce model size and complexity
Advantages: Reduced memory footprint; Faster inference; Lower power consumption; Compatible with existing RTOS
Disadvantages: May lose some accuracy; Requires model retraining; Limited to specific model architectures
Best Use Cases: Resource-constrained embedded systems; Battery-powered devices; Simple classification tasks

Description: Time-triggered execution and slack time utilization for ML tasks
Advantages: Maintains timing guarantees; No interference with critical tasks; Easier certification
Disadvantages: ML features available only during slack time; May underutilize ML capabilities; Requires careful timing analysis
Best Use Cases: Safety-critical systems; Mixed-criticality applications; Hard real-time requirements

Description: NPUs, FPGAs, DSPs for dedicated ML processing
Advantages: Deterministic execution times; High performance; Reduced CPU load; Energy efficient
Disadvantages: Higher cost; Increased complexity; May require specialized development tools
Best Use Cases: High-performance embedded systems; Automotive; Industrial automation

Description: RL-based or ML-driven dynamic scheduling policies
Advantages: Optimizes resource utilization; Adapts to workload changes; Improves overall system performance
Disadvantages: Non-deterministic behavior; Difficult to verify; May cause deadline misses during learning
Best Use Cases: Dynamic workloads; Soft real-time systems; Cloud-edge hybrid systems

Description: Anomaly detection and predictive maintenance using ML
Advantages: Proactive fault handling; Reduced downtime; Improved reliability; Preventive actions
Disadvantages: False positives; Requires training data; Additional monitoring overhead
Best Use Cases: Industrial IoT; Critical infrastructure; Long-running systems

Description: Combining deterministic RTOS with ML layers and safety monitors
Advantages: Balances adaptability and predictability; Fail-safe mechanisms; Easier certification
Disadvantages: More complex system design; Requires careful interface design; Higher development effort
Best Use Cases: Automotive; Medical devices; Aerospace; Safety-critical adaptive systems

Description: Offloading ML computation to edge or cloud resources
Advantages: Leverages powerful remote resources; Enables complex models; Easier updates
Disadvantages: Network latency; Connectivity dependency; Security concerns; Not suitable for hard real-time
Best Use Cases: IoT applications; Smart cities; Connected devices with relaxed timing

Description: Limiting ML execution with timeout mechanisms
Advantages: Prevents deadline violations; Maintains system stability; Simple implementation
Disadvantages: Incomplete inference; Reduced accuracy; Wasted computation if interrupted
Best Use Cases: Applications tolerating partial results; Best-effort ML services; Soft real-time

Description: Breaking ML computation into smaller chunks across time slots
Advantages: Spreads computational load; More predictable per-chunk timing; Flexible scheduling
Disadvantages: Requires state management; Increased memory for intermediate results; Not all models support this
Best Use Cases: Recurrent networks; Streaming applications; Pipelined processing
